data.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 2 advisory — both flag the deferred either/or choice). DECISION (resolving the advisory): collapse via approach (b)-as-delegation, NOT a raw delete. Rationale grounded in the code: the typed dispatch registry (_LEAVES, built from mutation._VALID_COMBINATIONS) and test_dispatch_coverage require EVERY valid (direction,action) — incl (outbound,update) — to route to a DISTINCT callable; a raw delete would make (outbound,update) raise NotImplementedError and break coverage. So _apply_outbound_update is rewritten as a thin DISTINCT adapter that converts the Mutation via _mutation_to_batch_dict and delegates to batch_dispatch.update_one — exactly mirroring the existing _apply_outbound_delete precedent. This removes the ~240-line PARALLEL apply implementation (the drift source) so outbound-update application has ONE source of truth (update_one); the dead _index_existing_links copy + dead _route_status_via_draft5 stub in apply_outbound.py are removed. Parity-first: a new combined test drives fields+comments+labels+links+parent through update_one asserting each sub-op takes effect, BEFORE the parallel logic is removed. Sub-op telemetry/canary (currently in the deleted typed-leaf payload) is intentionally NOT ported here — that is story E (per epic G6 sequencing); D's retargeted tests assert sub-op EFFECTS (client calls), not the old counter payload.
